name: Chocolate Raspberry Cake
recipeInstructions:
Cake:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Grease three 9-inch round cake pans and line with waxed paper.
3. Dust pans with flour, shaking off excess.
4. Stir together flour, 1/4 cup sugar, cocoa powder, salt, and baking powder.
5. In a large bowl, beat egg yolks, 1/2 cup sugar, and melted butter until thick and pale yellow.
6. Beat in vanilla extract.
7. In a medium bowl, beat egg whites until foamy.
8. Add remaining 1/4 cup sugar (1 tablespoon at a time) to the egg whites, beating until stiff but not dry peaks form.
9. Sift 1/3 of the flour mixture over the egg yolk mixture.
10. Beat in 1/3 of the egg whites.
11. Repeat steps 9-10 until all is mixed together.
12. Divide batter equally among three pans.
13. Bake for 15-18 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
14. Loosen cakes from the sides of the pans with a thin knife.
15. Immediately invert cakes onto wire racks to cool completely.
16. Remove waxed paper.
Frosting:
1. Melt chocolate and butter in the top of a double boiler over simmering water, stirring until smooth.
2. Cool completely.
3. In a large bowl, beat remaining 1 1/4 cups heavy cream, powdered sugar, and vanilla until soft peaks begin to form.
4. Do not beat frosting or it will become grainy.
5. Fold in 1/3 of the whipped cream into the chocolate mixture just until combined.
6. Fold the chocolate mixture into the remaining whipped cream just until combined.
7. Assemble by placing one layer on a serving plate and spreading with half the raspberry jam.
8. Spread about 2/3 cup frosting over the jam.
9. Stack the second cake layer on top and spread with the remaining jam and 2/3 cup frosting.
10. Place the third layer on top.
11. Frost the top and sides with the remaining jam and 2/3 cup frosting.
12. Decorate the top of the cake with fresh raspberries if desired.
13. Refrigerate, covered, until ready to serve
